'''Sean Penkywicz''' (born 18 May 1982) is a former [[Wales national rugby league team|Wales]] international [[rugby league]] footballer who last played as a {{rlp|hk}} for the [[Rochdale Hornets]] in [[RFL League 1]].

He has played at club level in the [[Super League]] for [[Halifax R.L.F.C.|Halifax]] (two spells, including the second in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]]), the [[Huddersfield Giants]], in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]] for the [[Leigh Centurions]],<ref name="They Played For Leigh (Statistics) at leighrl.co.uk">{{cite web|url=https://leighrl.co.uk/wp/they-played-for-leigh|title=They Played For Leigh (Statistics) at leighrl.co.uk|publisher=leighrl.co.uk|date=31 December 2017|accessdate=1 January 2018}}</ref><ref name="Heritage Numbers at leighrl.co.uk">{{cite web|url=https://leighrl.co.uk/wp/club/heritage-numbers|title=Heritage Numbers at leighrl.co.uk|publisher=leighrl.co.uk|date=31 December 2017|accessdate=1 January 2018}}</ref> in the inaugural 2017 season for the [[Toronto Wolfpack]], and for [[Workington Town]] in [[League 1 (rugby league)|Betfred League 1]], as a {{rlp|so|sh}} or {{rlp|hk}}.<ref name="Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/>

==Playing career==
Sean Penkywicz was born in [[Leigh, Greater Manchester|Leigh]], [[Greater Manchester]], England.

===Halifax RLFC===
Beginning his career with Halifax, Penkywicz made his Super League début in 2000.

===Huddersfield Giants===
He left Halifax and joined the [[Huddersfield Giants]] in 2004 following Halifax's relegation from the Super League. In 2005, Penkywicz was banned for two years after testing positive for steroid use.<ref>{{cite web |title=Penkywicz given two-year ban for steroid use |url=https://www.independent.co.uk/sport/general/rugby-league/penkywicz-given-twoyear-ban-for-steroid-use-495793.html |website=The Independent |access-date=8 July 2022 |date=21 April 2005}}</ref>

===Halifax RLFC (rejoined)===
In 2007 he rejoined Halifax in the Championship, where he went on to help the West Yorkshire outfit to a period of sustained success that included 2 Grand Final appearances in a row (2009 & 2010) and 2 Northern Rail Cup Final appearances in a row (2011 & 2012). Fax would win the Grand Final in 2010 beating Featherstone Rovers 23-22 before Penkywicz captained the blue and whites to Northern Rail Cup glory in 2012, again beating the lowly Rovers in a grandstand finale.

===Leigh Centurions===
After 5 seasons with Halifax, Penkywicz joined the Leigh Centurions in 2013. His contract was terminated in 2015 after failing a drugs test.<ref>{{cite web |title=Sean Penkywicz handed two-year doping ban |url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12196/9976202/sean-penkywicz-handed-two-year-doping-ban |website=Sky Sports |access-date=8 July 2022 |date=2 September 2015}}</ref>

===Toronto Wolfpack===
Penkywicz joined the [[Toronto Wolfpack]] on a short-term contract, making 13 appearances for the club during their inaugural 2017 season in [[League 1 (rugby league)|League 1]], and helping the team to promotion, after which he joined [[Workington Town]].

===Rochdale Hornets===
Penkywicz joined the [[Rochdale Hornets]] for the 2020 season and was appointed captain by coach [[Matt Calland]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.hornetsrugbyleague.co.uk/article/568/sean-penkywicz-%E2%80%98proud-of-captaincy-appointment|title=SEAN PENKYWICZ 'PROUD' OF CAPTAINCY APPOINTMENT|website=hornetsrugbyleague.co.uk|date=18 February 2020|accessdate=21 December 2020}}</ref>

==International==
In 2008, he was named in the [[Wales national rugby league team|Wales]] squad to face [[England national rugby league team|England]] at the [[Keepmoat Stadium]] prior to England's departure for the [[2008 Rugby League World Cup]].<ref name="Crusaders dominate Wales' squad">{{cite web|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport1/hi/rugby_league/7642490.stm|title=Crusaders dominate Wales' squad|publisher=BBC|date=3 October 2008|access-date=4 October 2008}}</ref>
